title: Alsophila celebica
intro: Alsophila celebica, also known as the Celebes tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to the island of Sulawesi in Indonesia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Alsophila celebica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, particularly those related to the skin and respiratory system.
taxonomy: Alsophila celebica is a species of tree fern in the family Cyatheaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of the Celebes Island in Indonesia. Alsophila celebica belongs to the genus Alsophila, which is a group of tree ferns commonly found in tropical regions. The species is classified as a vascular plant and is a member of the phylum Tracheophyta. Alsophila celebica is further classified as a flowering plant but non-flowering ferns belong to the phylum Pteridophyta.
